title: Transition-risk regime switching
plain_language_summary_zh: 研究状态切换附近的信号失效是否来自转移期不确定性，而非状态标签本身。
falsifiable_hypothesis: Signals observed near fixed regime-transition boundaries are less stable than signals well inside the same descriptive regimes on approved Development data.
proposed_market_mechanism: Competing participant horizons near state transitions may weaken signals defined for settled regimes.
supporting_evidence: Temporal generalization remains unresolved in the frozen research state.
contradictory_evidence: Transition labels may merely encode lag from the regime estimator.

source_refs.claim: The frozen state records unresolved temporal robustness questions.

novelty_vs_existing_research: Separates transition uncertainty from settled-regime behavior with fixed labels.
minimal_test_method: Compare fixed signal-stability labels inside transition windows and settled windows without fitting a strategy.
comparison_baseline: The same descriptive labels outside fixed transition windows.
falsification_conditions: Transition-window stability is not lower after controlling for fixed volatility bins.
stop_conditions: Stop after the pre-declared window comparison; do not optimize transition width.
known_limitations: Regime-transition labels are descriptive and estimator-dependent.
